title: Trees & BSTs
description: Hierarchical data structures in Python: Binary Trees, Binary Search Trees (BST), and depth-first traversals (Preorder, Inorder, Postorder).

sections.title: Binary Tree Node & Structure
sections.description: A Binary Tree is a hierarchical structure where each node has at most two children, referred to as left and right. Preorder, Inorder, and Postorder recursive functions are the classic depth-first traversal methods.
sections.codeBlock:
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self, val):
        self.val = val
        self.left = None
        self.right = None

# Create Tree:
#       1
#      / \
#     2   3
root = TreeNode(1)
root.left = TreeNode(2)
root.right = TreeNode(3)

# Traversals
def preorder(n, path):
    if n:
        path.append(n.val)
        preorder(n.left, path)
        preorder(n.right, path)

def inorder(n, path):
    if n:
        inorder(n.left, path)
        path.append(n.val)
        inorder(n.right, path)

def postorder(n, path):
    if n:
        postorder(n.left, path)
        postorder(n.right, path)
        path.append(n.val)

pre_path = []
preorder(root, pre_path)
print(pre_path)        # [1, 2, 3] (Root -> Left -> Right)

sections.title: Binary Search Tree (BST)
sections.description: A Binary Search Tree (BST) is a binary tree with ordering constraints: for any node, left descendants are smaller, and right descendants are larger. Inorder traversal of a BST yields elements in sorted order.

details:
Tree Traversals: Depth-First traversals visit tree nodes recursively. Preorder (Root-Left-Right) is commonly used to create copies of trees or evaluate prefix mathematical expressions. Inorder (Left-Root-Right) is used on BSTs to retrieve all keys in ascending order. Postorder (Left-Right-Root) is useful for post-fix expressions and deleting nodes (bottom-up execution ensures child nodes are deleted before parent nodes are cleaned).

BST Complexity: Average time complexities for search, insertion, and deletion are O(log N). However, if elements are inserted in sorted order, the tree degenerates into a linear chain (like a linked list), dropping execution speeds to O(N). Balancing structures (such as AVL trees or Red-Black trees) resolve this by self-balancing during insertions.
